_meta.board_id_source: video_automation/pinterest_boards.py — fitness / Workouts for Men Over 35
_meta.notes: 3 angles chosen to cover the buyer's three biggest objections: (1) it works at 40+, (2) you don't need 6 days/week, (3) recomposition is real.

pins.title: Lost 22 Pounds at 41 Without Running a Single Mile
pins.description:
Body recomposition after 35 is real, and it does not require endless cardio. The Blueprint is the exact 12-week strength plan that took me from soft and stuck to lean and strong. Three lifts per week. No cardio. Real food, no shakes. If you have lifted before but stalled, this is the system. Tap to see the full plan and the first week free.

#fitover35 #menshealth #musclebuilding #strengthtraining #fatlossafter35 #menover40 #bodyrecomposition #fitnessafter35
pins.image_query: muscular man over 40 lifting barbell home gym dramatic side lighting
tips: 22 lbs of fat lost without a single cardio session
Three full-body lifts per week, 45 minutes each
Protein target hit with real meals not shakes
Recovery weeks built in every fourth block
Joint friendly swaps for every heavy lift

pins.title: 5 Lifts That Build Muscle After 40 (Most Guys Skip 3)
pins.description:
Building muscle after 40 comes down to five lifts done well, not twenty done badly. These are the exact movements inside the Blueprint that put 9 pounds of lean mass on a 43-year-old desk worker in 12 weeks. Joint friendly variations included for every lift. Save this for your next session and tap for the full progression chart.

#muscleafter35 #menover40 #fitover35 #strengthtraining #musclebuilding #bodyrecomposition #mensfitness #workoutover35
pins.image_query: man over 40 deadlift barbell gym strong back muscle definition
tips: Trap bar deadlift for safer lower back loading
Incline dumbbell press for shoulder friendly chest
Goblet squat to relearn pattern before you load
Single arm row to fix decades of desk posture
Loaded carry to build the grip and core nobody trains

pins.title: The 3-Day Lifting Split for Busy Men Over 35
pins.description:
If you cannot train six days a week and refuse to give up your weekends, this is for you. The Blueprint is built around three 45-minute sessions per week. No two-a-days, no fasted cardio, no nonsense. Designed for men with a job, a family, and 35+ years on the body. Tap for the full 12-week schedule and the recovery rules that make it work.

#fitover35 #mensfitness #strengthtraining #busymendad #workoutover35 #menover40 #musclebuilding #bodyrecomposition

tips: Three full body sessions per week, 45 minutes each
